text.title: claude-best-practices Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for claude-best-practices as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Cache hit requires identical prefix — any runtime injection (timestamps, request IDs) placed before the cached block breaks the cache silently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The cache mechanism uses byte-for-byte prefix matching. Any character difference, even whitespace, causes a cache miss. Dynamic content must always come after the cached block.

failure-mode.description: RateLimitError from Anthropic includes a retry-after header, but guessing the backoff interval instead of parsing it.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The API provides the exact wait time. Guessing may be too conservative (wasting throughput) or too aggressive (getting rate-limited again).

failure-mode.description: Fallback to Haiku on rate limiting is invisible to the orchestrator; downstream agents may produce worse outputs without knowing why.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The orchestrator cannot adjust for quality degradation if it doesn't know which model actually ran. Always log the model used.

failure-mode.description: Tool schemas with additionalProperties: false reject unexpected keys; LLMs sometimes add extra fields — strip unknown keys before passing tool results back.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The API validates against the schema. Invalid keys cause tool use to fail. Strip them before returning the result to the model.

failure-mode.description: Extended thinking (thinking param) blocks count toward output tokens but are non-cacheable; large think budgets can 5-10x the cost of a single call.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Thinking tokens bypass the cache and are expensive. Budget them carefully for truly complex reasoning tasks only.

failure-mode.description: Human-in-loop checkpoint: when stop_reason == "max_tokens" on a structured output call, the JSON is truncated and invalid — do not parse it; retry with higher max_tokens.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Truncated JSON will fail parsing. The correct action is to retry with more headroom, not to handle the parse error.
